title: Xenial Syrian Rice Pilaf
description: A buttery, shaggy rice pilaf infused with Grand Marnier, blanched spinach, and toasted pistachios for a romantic, dairy-free twist.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup long-grain white rice under cold water until the water runs clear.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat; add onion, carrot, celery and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, and turmeric and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add Grand Marnier, stir to deglaze the pan, and let the alcohol evaporate for 2 minutes.
Pour in 2 cups vegetable broth, add bay leaf, salt, pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until rice is tender, about 18 minutes, then remove from heat and let stand covered 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, blend blanched spinach with 1 teaspoon sesame oil, a pinch of salt, and a squeeze of lemon juice; set aside.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and fold in chopped apricots, toasted pistachios, and the spinach mixture.
Garnish with additional pistachios and a drizzle of Grand Marnier, if desired.
Serve immediately as a buttery, shaggy pilaf with a romantic touch.
